mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, VEGFR, and PDGFR to reduce survival and proliferation signaling
additional_notes: Inhibits FLT3-ITD AML lines including MOLM13

mechanism: Inhibits SRC family kinases and ABL, disrupting oncogenic signaling pathways
additional_notes: Shows antiproliferative effects in subsets of AML via SRC family and other kinases

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and ABL tyrosine kinases, affecting downstream proliferative pathways
additional_notes: Has off-target activity in some AML models; considered as a repurposing candidate

mechanism: Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2 and BCL-XL, triggering apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as ABT-263; induces apoptosis in AML cell lines; more toxic due to BCL-XL but a useful in vitro tool compound

mechanism: Blocks JAK1 and JAK2, dampening JAK/STAT signaling involved in AML cell survival
additional_notes: JAK/STAT signaling contributes to some AML subtypes; reported synergy with other agents

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK3, reducing cytokine-dependent signaling pathways
additional_notes: Repurposing interest in hematologic malignancies due to modulation of cytokine signaling

mechanism: Inhibits multiple histone deacetylases, leading to altered chromatin structure and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; strong antiproliferative and pro-apoptotic effects in AML cell lines, including FLT3-ITD models; many AML IC50 values in low-nanomolar range

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Induces apoptosis in AML cell lines including FLT3-ITD; strong preclinical evidence; can serve as a positive cytotoxic control at 1 µM

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, disrupting protein homeostasis and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Highly potent irreversible proteasome inhibitor; induces apoptosis in AML preclinical models

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, disturbing redox balance and increasing ROS to kill leukemia cells
additional_notes: Reported selective toxicity in AML via ROS and redox imbalance; considered a repurposing candidate in leukemia

mechanism: Inhibits ALDH and forms a Cu(DDC)₂ complex in presence of copper, affecting proteasome activity and ROS generation
additional_notes: Shown to kill AML stem/progenitor cells in vitro; repurposing interest; activity can be enhanced with copper (e.g., CuCl₂ 0.5–2 µM kept constant across wells)

category: AMPK activator / mitochondrial complex I modulator
mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and activates AMPK, causing energetic stress and secondary mTOR inhibition
additional_notes: Anti-leukemic effects generally require higher micromolar–millimolar levels in vitro; focus practically on 0.5–10 mM due to solubility and off-target issues

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and blocks autophagic flux, impairing AML cell survival and sensitizing to other drugs
additional_notes: Both chloroquine and hydroxychloroquine act as autophagy inhibitors; moderate single-agent effects at higher micromolar concentrations; often used in combination studies
